ruleTypes.HARD RULE: Enforced by AO3 sanitizer, parser, runtime, or config.
ruleTypes.BASELINE: Current AO3 default style, DOM, class, or behavior; useful to match or override.
ruleTypes.RECOMMENDATION: Safer authoring or userscript practice; valid alternatives may exist.
ruleTypes.REFERENCE: Lookup data such as tokens, sprite coordinates, constants, selectors, or routing notes.

docs.readWhen: Orienting to guide purpose, deployment, or choosing which docs to load.
Before writing AO3 CSS, validating sanitizer compatibility, sizing with ems, using variables, or debugging stripped CSS.

sections.readWhen: Checking allowed CSS properties and values.
Debugging duplicate declarations, font shorthand, or parser behavior.

docs.readWhen: Looking up AO3 default colors, fonts, button states, or visual tokens. These are not constraints.
Choosing selectors, understanding AO3 class vocabulary, or detecting page/state classes.
Reasoning about cascade order, user skin placement, z-index, breakpoints, or responsive overrides.
Selecting AO3 DOM nodes, parsing blurbs/meta/comments/forms, or checking AO3 config constants.
Detecting AO3 JS-managed state classes, dropdowns, filter panels, modals, widgets, or validation state.
Calling AO3 modal APIs, hooking Rails AJAX events, using global functions, or waiting for CSRF.
Working with required-tags icons, sprite coordinates, bookmark/comment icons, or icon replacement.
Using built-in AO3 work skin utility classes or scoping work skin CSS to #workskin.
